issues.title: Keyboard disappears when TextField is dynamically wrapped
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run the below code
2. Tap the `TextField`
3. Enter a character
4. Observe that the keyboard immediately disappears and the entered character "disappears" (I expect that the keyboard remains visible and the entered character appears in the `TextField`)

NOTE: I used a `Dismissible` in my repro because that's what I'm using in my real app. However, the problem is still present if I wrap it in something else instead, like a `Container`.

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(new MyApp());

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return new MaterialApp(
      title: 'Repro',
      home: Repro(),
    );
  }
}

class Repro extends StatefulWidget {
  @override
  _ReproState createState() => _ReproState();
}

class _ReproState extends State<Repro> {
  final Key _textFieldKey = UniqueKey();
  final Key _dismissibleKey = UniqueKey();
  bool _wrapInDismissible = false;

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    final children = <Widget>[];

    final textField = TextField(
      key: _textFieldKey,
      onChanged: (value) => setState(() => _wrapInDismissible = value != ""),
    );

    if (!_wrapInDismissible) {
      children.add(textField);
    } else {
      final dismissible = Dismissible(
        key: _dismissibleKey,
        child: textField,
      );
      children.add(dismissible);
    }

    var column = Column(
      children: children,
    );

    var scaffold = Scaffold(appBar: AppBar(title: Text("Repro")),
    body: column,);

    return scaffold;
  }
}
```

comments: I understand that this might be due to using `UniqueKey` instead of `GlobalKey` (haven't verified yet). Even so, I'd like to understand _why_ that is. The documentation for `GlobalKey` discourages using it frivolously, and this felt like an instance where I could get away with a lighter weight key. Given that the key is unique and doesn't change, why isn't that sufficient?

I don't think using GlobalKey is bad if you use it sparingly. If you create a lot you're probably using them wrongly anyway. GlobalKey allows you to access the state of a widget from anywhere, UniqueKey is more for Flutter to be able to figure out what widgets (not) to reuse when rebuilding (from my limited knowledge about this topic)

A StackOverflow question would probably give you good feedback faster.
Have you tried passing a custom `FocusNode` to `TextField` that you keep alive between builds?
Is this still an issue?

> Have you tried passing a custom `FocusNode` to `TextField` that you keep alive between builds?

I can confirm that this doesn't work.

Also, if I change _textFieldKey from a UniqueKey to a GlobalKey, the problem goes away, but I'm not sure if that's considered bad practice

issues.title: [Web]: TextField `undo` option from selection bar is not available after the TextField loses focus
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Execute `flutter run -d chrome --web-renderer html` on the code sample <!-- (see "Code sample" section below) -->
2. Delete some text from the TextField. At this point, one can undo changes.
3. Make another change in the TextField, and then click on the page outside the TextField, so that it loses focus.
4. Click back into the TextField. Undo is not available any more.

**Expected results:**
I expect to be able to undo changes to the TextField regardless of whether it lost focus or not.

**Actual results:**
One can undo changes as long as focus hasn't been lost, but not once focus has left the TextField.

**Notes:**
This only happens when built for web; desktop is fine. Inspecting #shadow-root, I suspect the HTML renderer is recreating the `<input>` element (or one of its ancestors) when the TextField receives focus, thereby losing the undo history from the original `<input>`.

<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(const MyApp());

class MyApp extends StatefulWidget {
  const MyApp({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  @override
  State<MyApp> createState() => _MyAppState();
}

class _MyAppState extends State<MyApp> {
  final _controller = TextEditingController(text: 'Edit me, then change focus, then try to undo...');

  @override
  void dispose() {
    _controller.dispose();
    super.dispose();
  }

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        backgroundColor: Colors.white,
        body: SizedBox(
          width: 420,
          child: Column(
            mainAxisSize: MainAxisSize.min,
            children: [
              const Text('Running on web with HTML renderer'),
              TextField(
                controller: _controller,
              ),
            ],
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

</details>

<details>
  <summary>Logs</summary>

<!--
      Run your application with `flutter run --verbose` and attach all the
      log output below between the lines with the backticks. If there is an
      exception, please see if the error message includes enough information
      to explain how to solve the issue.
-->

comments:
Thanks for the report. I verified this behavior and observed that it happens using canvaskit too, so it isn't specific to html rendering. Also, to reproduce this issue, we need to make use to selection bar which is shown after right clicking on the textfield. Below are steps to replicate: 1. Run code sample provided. 2. Tap on textfield to make it active. 3. Select any existing word from the textfield and use (cmd + x) from keyboard shortcut to cut the text. 4. Tap outside of the textfield so that it loses focus. 5. Tap inside textfield to gain focus. 6. right click on the textfield so that selection bar shows up and observe that `undo` is disabled. 7. Without tapping outside of the textfield and if we right click, then observe that `undo` is enabled and we can undo the operation successfully. Also, I confirmed that this is specific to web because when I tried on desktop, the selection bar is not available on it, so considering this as a web specific issue.

Thanks for verifying this @darshankawar! I think I can add a clarification:

It's really the undo functionality that's the issue, rather than the right-click context menu/selection bar. Ctrl-Z does not work to undo either. Showing the context menu was just a means to visually show that the undo-history has been lost. On desktop, Ctrl-Z works fine even after having lost focus.

~~I think it ultimately really is a HTML renderer issue, in the sense that I think the CanvasKit renderer is falling back to using HTML `<input>` elements because the sample code is so simple.~~ Edit: It looks like even the CanvasKit renderer uses `<input>` elements for TextFields, and does the same thing of recreating them in the shadow DOM, thereby losing the history. (I accidentally built in Linux when trying to test the CanvasKit version, so didn't see this! 😁)

This must be the side-effect of the web engine creating a new `<input>` element after the focus is lost. We currently lack signals from the framework about what text fields exist in the UI other than the focused ones, with the exception perhaps of text fields grouped into a form. This needs some thought. One quick and dirty fix might be to cache `<input>` elements and reuse them. It will be a little unpredictable because we can't maintain an infinite cache, and will need to clear elements from it eventually, but it might be good enough?

Personally I never have more than 10 TextField-style `<input>`s per page, and I wouldn't expect the undo-history to be preserved across page changes, so @yjbanov's caching suggestion sounds good to me.

issues.title: Flutter 3.7 breaks TextField widgets that wrap MaterialApp.router
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->
After updating to Flutter 3.7, when a TextField gains focus, the keyboard appears but then immediately is dismissed. This issue was originally reported [here](https://github.com/wiredashio/wiredash-sdk/issues/262). I have confirmed that this issue does not occur in Flutter v3.3. This bug only occurs when a Router is used and only seems to occur for TextFields that are outside the `MaterialApp` in the widget tree. It is _not_ specific to the [wiredash](https://pub.dev/packages/wiredash) package. I've tested it with [flappy](https://pub.dev/packages/flappy) and the same thing happens.

1. Download the [Wiredash sample repo](https://github.com/percula/wiredash_test)
2. Run the sample on an iOS or Android device. There is no need to provide the Wiredash `projectId` or `secret`.
3. Tap the FAB to bring up the Wiredash interface
4. Try to type in the `TextField`

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
The keyboard will appear and remain.

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->
The keyboard appears and immediately dismisses.

comments: @passsy Would you be able to create a super-simple sample that replicates what Wiredash is doing? I tried, but apparently it's not as simple as wrapping the app in a column with a `TextField` above it 😉.

@darshankawar I can confirm that the issue no longer exists on the latest master. I'm looking through the commits on `Navigator` to see if anything looks like it might have fixed it. If we can identify the fix, any chance this could go into a hotfix on the stable channel?

Awesome, that's very good news!

You should be able to identify the commit with `git bisect`. Once we know the commit I can ask it to be cherry-picked. Since it is a regression and the release was last week we have good chances to get it approved.
The commit that fixes this issue is a45727d81360b278cc51c5a9ae429529038cf069.
Makes sense, because Wiredash wraps the app with `MediaQuery`. Removing the WidgetsApps dependency on `MediaQuery`, prevents it from rebuilding and requesting focus.

@passsy This is interesting, when I'm on `master` (or add the lines from the [gist](https://gist.github.com/percula/53931260f13d2d34512e30ee37e8d835) I created) in my sample project with Wiredash, the issue does not occur, even after updating the sample to use a `StatefulWidget` as you mentioned in the Wiredash issue. However, when I use your simplified code above, I get the same result as you.

Thanks for providing the updated and a minimal reproducible code sample. Using it, I was able to verify that the functionality is not working on latest stable and master, but works as expected on previous stable (3.3.10).

Wiredash now always wraps the app in a `FocusScope`, as `WidgetsApp` did before. Does that mean that Wiredash users will run into similar issues with Nested Routing?

Okay, can you file a new issue for that?  I don't think I'll be able to tell exactly what the issue is until I know the details of how you're using it.

As for the nested navigator issue, it mainly comes up when there are two panels, each with their own navigator: before I removed the `FocusScope` from `Navigator`, it was impossible to use tab traversal between the two panels because you'd get trapped in one scope.  I think as long as you don't wrap more than one app, it'll probably be fine for your customers: the problem arose because there were two `FocusScope`s in that scenario, and you should only ever have one.
Please I still have the same issue, I’m using flutter 3.10

> Please I still have the same issue, I’m using flutter 3.10

I'm still using Flutter 3.3 due to this bug 😅
Omg It should have been resolved by now , do you know how I can downgrade to v3.3
Hi, is there going to be a solution soon on this, it still doesn't work for the last few major updates.
Any news about this? Spent the whole day trying to make my Flutter app working again without success.

What a pathetic work done by the flutter go router team!. I'm on 3.16.0. There is no way downgrade my flutter to 3.3.10.

Is there a way or should i look for React?

@puspharaj-teachspot I understand you are frustrated, but please re-read our [code of conduct](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md?rgh-link-date=2023-11-16T12%3A26%3A42Z) in regard to respectful and welcoming communication.
I added global navigatorKey for GoRouter and this solved issue for me.
This issue is missing a priority label. Please set a priority label when adding the `triaged-design` label.

issues.title: TextField bug when contentPadding is not zero and there is a high hint message
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample on Linux or web platform target
2. Follow the on screen instructions (or their duplicate below).
2.1. Ensure "behavior is bad" mode on (toggle button).
2.2. Resize window height and confirm that hint text remains inside the TextField widget. (on Android devices rotation of a device will emulate the resize)
2.3. Make window height large.
2.4. Type some value inside the TextField.
2.5. Resize window height and confirm that typed in text remains inside the TextField widget.
2.6. Erase typed in text completely to make hint text visible again.
2.7. Make window height large.
2.8. Turn on "behavior is owfull" mode.
2.9. Resize window height and confirm that hint text goes outside of the TextField widget.
2.10. Make window height large.
2.11. Type some value inside the TextField.
2.12. Resize window height and confirm that the typed in text goes outside of the TextField widget. That's it. The bug!

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
Hint text remains inside of TextField widget area.
Input text and input focus remains inside of TextField widget area

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->
Hint text and input text appear on other widgets area.

The problem appear on Linux, web and Android platforms. Haven't checked other platforms.

<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>

<!--
      Please create a minimal reproducible sample that shows the problem
      and attach it below between the lines with the backticks.

      To create it you can use `flutter create bug` command and update the `main.dart` file.

      Alternatively, you can use https://dartpad.dev/
      which is capable of creating and running small Flutter apps.

      Without this we will unlikely be able to progress on the issue, and because of that
      we regretfully will have to close it.
-->

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatefulWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  State<MyApp> createState() => _MyAppState();
}

class _MyAppState extends State<MyApp> {
  bool _showBug = false;

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    const String title =
        'Flutter TextField with decoration (border + hintText) bug';
    const double nonZeroBorderWidth = 50;
    InputBorder border = const OutlineInputBorder(
      borderSide: BorderSide(
        color: Colors.orange,
        width: nonZeroBorderWidth,
      ),
    );

    return MaterialApp(
      title: title,
      theme: ThemeData(
        primaryColor: Colors.red,
      ),
      home: Scaffold(
        appBar: AppBar(
          title: const Text(title),
        ),
        body: Column(
          crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start,
          children: <Widget>[
            ColoredBox(
              color: Colors.red,
              child: Row(
                mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.end,
                children: [
                  ToggleButtons(
                    onPressed: (index) {
                      setState(() {
                        _showBug = !_showBug;
                      });
                    },
                    color: Colors.black,
                    selectedColor: Colors.black,
                    selectedBorderColor: Colors.black,
                    isSelected: [_showBug],
                    children: [
                      Text(_showBug
                          ? 'Behavior is owfull when contentPadding={top:$nonZeroBorderWidth}. TextField.hintText and/or input is outside of the TextField widget when there is no enough space to show TextField.hintText.'
                          : 'Behavior is bad when contentPadding={top:0}. TextField.hintText and/or input text is over the orange border. But at least that text is inside the TextField widget.'),
                    ],
                  ),
                ],
              ),
            ),
            Expanded(
              child: TextField(
                decoration: InputDecoration(
                  border: border,
                  enabledBorder: border,
                  focusedBorder: border,
                  hintText: '''hint line 1. Ensure "behavior is bad" mode on.
hint line 2. Resize window height and confirm that hint text remains inside the TextField widget.
hint line 3. Make window height large.
hint line 4. Type some value inside the TextField.
hint line 5. Resize window height and confirm that typed in text remains inside the TextField widget.
hint line 6.
hint line 7. Make window height large.
hint line 8. Turn on "behavior is owfull" mode.
hint line 9. Resize window height and confirm that hint text goes outside of the TextField widget.
hint line 10. Make window height large.
hint line 11. Type some value inside the TextField.
hint line 12. Resize window height and confirm that the typed in text goes outside of the TextField widget.
hint line 13
hint line 14
hint line 15
hint line 16
hint line 17
hint line 18
hint line 19
hint line 20''',
                  contentPadding: EdgeInsets.only(top: (_showBug ? nonZeroBorderWidth : 0)),
                  filled: true,
                  fillColor: Colors.yellow,
```

issues.title: Crash while type some characters and double click in textfield: 'NSInternalInconsistencyException', reason: 'Expected a valid text range.'
issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. type "hdjdd" 
2. double click textfield
3. crash

work in iOS 17.0.0 well. While update to 17.1.1 crash.
### Expected results

no crash

### Actual results


*** Terminating app due to uncaught exception 'NSInternalInconsistencyException', reason: 'Expected a valid text range.'
*** First throw call stack:
(0x1a277c870 0x19aa97c00 0x1a1ce6d18 0x10af72f54 0x1a5560928 0x1a555ec90 0x1a5557724 0x1a55573ec 0x1a55539ac 0x1a54d37e0 0x1a4bc3c2c 0x1a4bc3b10 0x1a551b738 0x1a54d34a0 0x10af71a78 0x10af79d04 0x10b4fe1c8 0x10af6b590 0x10a678f50 0x10a67ab34 0x10a68afec 0x10a68aba0 0x1a26c720c 0x1a26c3f18 0x1a26c3668 0x1e5a8e5ec 0x1a4ae02b4 0x1a4adf8f0 0x104ae49cc 0x1c5116dcc)



### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  const MyHomePage({super.key, required this.title});

  final String title;

  @override
  State<MyHomePage> createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {
 
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(
        title: Text(widget.title),
      ),
      body: const Center(
        child: TextField(),
      ),
    );
  }
}

```

</details>

@dam-ease 
my device is 13 PRO MAX.
I reorganized it and found that the issue seems to be like this
For example, input "hdjdd" and then wait for a moment to let the iOS keyboard generate predictive suggestions. At this point, the text inside the textfield will be automatically selected, and the suggestion below is "hdjk". Now, double-click the TextField and click "Select All."

I suspect that because the length of "hdjk" is smaller than "hdjdd," it causes a crash. So, the issue seems to be that when inputting certain characters, if the length of the suggested word is shorter than the original one, it leads to a crash.
type "fggjiy" ，the suggested is "fight"，and it crash


https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/113404168/00f28d97-0034-4b60-8a05-a45b2c5faba4

Thanks for your response. I tried these as well but can't reproduce the crash, maybe it could be device-specific, can you also try these steps on an emulator of a different device with the same OS?
Also, kindly help with the full output of your `flutter run -v`?

I have information from users of my app with the same problem, but I couldn't reproduce it because I don't have a device with iOS 17+, but I have videos recorded by customers and the same thing happens, when interacting with a textField the app freezes for a few moments and closes in followed.

> I have information from users of my app with the same problem, but I couldn't reproduce it because I don't have a device with iOS 17+, but I have videos recorded by customers and the same thing happens, when interacting with a textField the app freezes for a few moments and closes in followed.

I managed to minimize the problem by setting the "autocorrect: false" attribute.

Although it's not the best of all worlds, at least the app stopped crashing.

Thanks for responding @AguinaldoAranda.
Can you help with the full output of your `flutter run -v`?

> Thanks for responding @AguinaldoAranda. Can you help with the full output of your `flutter run -v`?

Hello.

The problem was reported by a customer, iPhone 11 with iOS 17.1.2

The problem does not happen in my development environment, with an iPhone 11/14 simulator and iOS 16.2.

And although the problem is related to inputText, in my case, the app closes when the user presses to use a text suggestion.

I noticed that the attr "autocorrect: false," does not disable the suggestions if the attr TextInputType is text, I changed it to TextInputType = name, this way it worked, but very frustrating.

Sorry for English.

Thanks for your response.
I can't seem to reproduce this as well on my end.
I'll keep this open and label for more insights from the team.
Thank you!

> Thanks for your response. I can't seem to reproduce this as well on my end. I'll keep this open and label for more insights from the team. Thank you!

Hello, if I find any more concrete evidence I will return here, we have 2 apps showing this behavior, several reports from customers.

instead of throwing it looks like our [`text(in:)`](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uitextinput/1614527-text) implementation should return `nil` instead when the input range has NSNotFound.

Hey I am facing this issue on iOS 17+ device, when AutoCorrect is enabled. And as mention by @AguinaldoAranda the app is freezing and crashing 2 or 3 times initially. I added flags enabledSuggestions and autoCorrect to false but that seems to not working as expected

> Hey I am facing this issue on iOS 17+ device, when AutoCorrect is enabled. And as mention by @AguinaldoAranda the app is freezing and crashing 2 or 3 times initially. I added flags enabledSuggestions and autoCorrect to false but that seems to not working as expected

I have had success with autocorrect:false when used in conjunction with attr keyboardType: TextInputType.name

issues.title: [Android] TextField cursor doesn't move to tapped position, but converts into text selection mode after selecting input mode from virtual keyboard.
issues.body:
**UPDATE:** For current status, see https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/98720#issuecomment-1548704965 and thereafter.

.

.

Original report follows.

---

I found an issue with TextField, I'm not sure if it's intended or not.
After entering text in the TextField, if you select the text in the middle, the cursor should move to that position.
However, if you add two exclamation marks after the text and select the middle of the text, the cursor does not move to that location, but selects up to that location.

This issue was found in both English and Korean.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Enter arbitrary strings in the TextField.
2. Finally, enter two exclamation marks.
3. Click in the middle of the text.
4. Instead of moving the cursor to that position, it is selected from the back to the selected point.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/26322627/154611611-48731563-9fa1-4270-b1d7-7aebfe5e0a1c.mp4


<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>

<!--
      Please create a minimal reproducible sample that shows the problem
      and attach it below between the lines with the backticks.

      To create it you can use `flutter create bug` command and update the `main.dart` file.

      Alternatively, you can use https://dartpad.dev/
      which is capable of creating and running small Flutter apps.

      Without this we will unlikely be able to progress on the issue, and because of that
      we regretfully will have to close it.
-->

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: Column(
          mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
          children: const [
            Padding(
              padding: EdgeInsets.all(8.0),
              child: TextField(),
            ),
          ],
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

</details>

<details>
  <summary>flutter doctor -v</summary>

<!-- Finally, paste the output of running `flutter doctor -v` here. -->

To make an issue actionable it has to be as precise as possible. It is not always easy, because some issues look very similar.
In this case, I think that @hhkk comments are about a different issue which is a Flutter web issue (see https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/124483).

The original issue is an Android one only reproducible on some Samsung devices.

From https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/98720#issuecomment-1498550302, it seems that something was fixed on Samsung's side. Collecting precise information related to devices and Android version showing this issue would be useful.

@bleroux The very issue you mention gives very precise information as to when the issue occurs ? There are multiple comments give Flutter version, Android version and device. How many do we need before the issue gets investigated further ?

> Is it helpful for us to itemize the issues we are seeing

Definitely, yes. There is active work all the time on text input in Flutter, and high-quality reproducible bug reports are very helpful in getting issues fixed. For a sample of ongoing work, see the "a: text input" label, in particular [recently closed issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues?q=label%3A%22a%3A+text+input%22+is%3Aissue+is%3Aclosed+sort%3Aupdated-desc) and [recently merged PRs](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues?q=label%3A%22a%3A+text+input%22+is%3Amerged+sort%3Aupdated-desc).

Please do itemize separate issues into separate threads. When a thread starts having a mixture of several different issues, as this one has, it makes it harder to make progress on any of them.

Summarizing the state of the issue:

It affects Samsung Keyboard versions before 5.x, which means Android 10 and older: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/98720#issuecomment-1498550302, https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/98720#issuecomment-1046616633 from @trongnhantaisacventoan, @darshankawar

The key step in the repro isn't typing the exclamation marks, but going to the symbols pane of the keyboard where they appear: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/98720#issuecomment-1144789254, https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/98720#issuecomment-1159125528 from @inevitabile, @LongCatIsLooong 

When hitting the key to switch to the symbols pane, the sequence of key events we emit seems wrong: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/98720#issuecomment-1159125528 from @LongCatIsLooong

A workaround which seems to work (and may be informative for a proper fix) is to inject key-up events for the shift key: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/98720#issuecomment-1501036442 from @Johann673

A different workaround, within the Flutter framework, is to remove a bit of code in `text_selection.dart`: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/98720#issuecomment-1533381070 from @whitesoil

---

Looking at that last bit of code:
```dart
    if (isShiftPressedValid) {
      _isShiftTapping = true;
      _extendSelection(details.globalPosition, SelectionChangedCause.tap);
      return;
    }
```

one thing I see is that it looks like we're responding as if the Shift key were held, and carrying out the behavior that would be correct if Shift were held: extending the selection. The problem, then, is that we wrongly think Shift is held.

That's consistent with the puzzling extra Shift key-down event that @LongCatIsLooong found at https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/98720#issuecomment-1159125528, and with Shift key-up events as a workaround as found by @Johann673.

So I think the next debugging question is why we have that spurious key-down event for Shift. And if we fix that, that should resolve this issue.

One more bit: #105185 (by @inevitabile) has some other repro details that may be helpful:
* The broken state can be entered not only by hitting the switch-to-symbols key, but also the comma key `,`.

  These are the two keys at the bottom left: they make up the bottom row to the left of the spacebar. The switch-to-symbols key appears directly below the Shift key.

* The broken state can be exited not only by hitting Shift (which makes sense given the diagnosis that we're stuck thinking Shift is held down), but also by hitting Backspace. OTOH basically nothing else works for exiting the broken state:

  > You fall into a trap. Typing, closing the keyboard, setting the focus on another TextField - nothing helps. The cursor is still stuck.

  The Backspace key is at the right end of the next-to-bottom row, while the Shift key is at the left end of the same row.

The role of Backspace is intriguing because it's located at the mirror image of where Shift is — it's where a RightShift key would be if there were one. I wonder if we're somehow ending up with a RightShift (or LeftShift) key-up event from it, and if that's how it ends the broken state.

It'd also be interesting to know how the comma key `,` gets us into the broken state. Is it also somehow producing a spurious Shift key-down event?

I reproduce on a Samsung device running Android 6 and Samsung keyboard 1.5.46. A couple of observations there:

---

On this keyboard, unlike the image at #105185, the key between "symbols" and space is a settings key (with a gear icon) instead of a comma key `,`.

Nevertheless, that key *also* reproduces the issue, just like the comma key in the same location does in the report #105185. To reproduce:
* Have some text typed.
* Hit the settings key. This opens the settings in a separate activity.
* Return to the app, either through the app switcher or by navigating back.
* The input is now in the broken state this issue is about: tapping extends the selection instead of placing the caret, just as if Shift were held.

Also just like in the report #105185, either Backspace or Shift exits the broken state, but pretty much nothing else does: typing, closing the keyboard, unfocusing, switching to another app and back, all preserve it.

---

In contrast with @whitesoil's finding at https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/98720#issuecomment-1307471499, the issue reproduces regardless of the value of the "Keyboard swipe" setting: that setting can be "Cursor control", "Continuous input", or "None", and the issue reproduces all the same.

@gnprice Thanks a lot!

As you mentioned, Event synthesization is done on the engine side (see https://github.com/NevercodeHQ/engine/blob/main/shell/platform/android/io/flutter/embedding/android/KeyEmbedderResponder.java).

The issue here seems to be that the Samsung keyboard emits a key event for Shift right, `keycode = 60`, with a non zero `metaState` (65) which is the equivalent of holding the shift key.

FYI, IME usually do not emit such keyboard events (and Samsung possibly fix this on their newer version where it does not reproduce). 

For the moment, I see two paths for a fix:
- It is possible that there is a bug on the engine side about Shift Left/Shift Right.
- if there is no bug, it might be possible to handle separatly this specific case (keycode = 60, metaState = 65). But we will have to take care not to break external keyboard usage.

In the meantime, the workaround in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/98720#issuecomment-1501036442 looks good (it might be a problem with external keyboards but it is not certain).

I have tried this simple step, and it works for me:
Go into **Setting -> .... -> Samsung Keyboard ->Swipe, touch, and feedback -> Keyboard swipe controls -> Select "No swipe gestures"**

Hi @dkwingsmt could you take a look and see if the synthesized key event is expected? It seems there's no corresponding up event for the synthesized down event and the application thinks the user is hold down the <kbd>shift</kbd> modifier the whole time.

Yeah it's expected that every key down event should be followed by an up event, either of them synthesized or not. 

From what I read from @/gnprice 's analysis, it seems like the onscreen keyboard software emits a ShiftRight down, but didn't emit a corresponding ShiftRight up. Although normally with hardware keyboard this will be addressed at the next key event by synchronizing on meta state, immediately tapping after it will operate in the Shift mode. 

A way to fix it would be to force a metaState synchronization before text events. But I'll need someone to test if it really fixes the problem, since I don't have access to testing devices.
Any plans for the fix?

Hi all. Any update on this?

I've recently noticed that this issue is easily reproducible on ANY app made with Flutter (e.g. `NotebookLM`) in combination with `Gboard`'s "select word and tap Shift to capitalize" feature. And since I use this feature a lot, I actually started to hate using apps made with Flutter (including my own ones)... I really hope this will get resolved soon...

### Steps to reproduce:
1. Use Android phone with `Gboard` installed
2. Open any app written in Flutter and focus on any TextField (e.g. NotebookLM > Create New)
3. Type `Hello world`
4. Double tap on `world` to select
5. Tap Shift to capitalize
6. Tap on `Hello` and observe (unwanted) permanent selection mode

### Tested on:
- Google Pixel 7 Pro, Android 16
- Gboard version: 17.0.12.880768217-release-arm64-v8a com.google.android.inputmethod.latin
- NotebookLM version: 1.32.7.889108720 com.google.android.apps.labs.language.tailwind
